project.creator: Codex
environment: 1. Frontend: React admin audit-log feature in client/src/features/audit-logs
3. Backend audit-log module inspected for matching export endpoint
4. Evidence source: code inspection only; repository tests were not executed because installed dependencies are unavailable in this workspace
project.notes: FN-23 Admin Log Export was selected because docs did not contain a completed function-level workbook pair for this feature. Current code implements two partial export paths: auditLogsApi.export prepares a GET /audit-logs/export blob request, and AuditLogPage.handleExport downloads the currently loaded logs as client-side JSON. The backend currently exposes only GET /audit-logs and does not implement /audit-logs/export, so end-to-end server export cases remain draft and untested by design.

changeLog.description: Initial function-level workbook generated from the implemented client export flow and the documented backend export gap.

features.name: Admin Log Export
features.testRequirement: Validate the implemented admin audit-log export helpers, the current browser JSON download flow, and the documented failure or gap when a server-side /audit-logs/export endpoint is expected but not implemented.

functions.name: Audit Logs Export API Wrapper
functions.description: Prepare a blob download request to GET /audit-logs/export with optional audit-log filters.
functions.preCondition: The audit-log frontend module is loaded and the shared API client is available.

functions.name: Audit Log Page Client Export
functions.description: Serialize the currently loaded audit-log rows into JSON and trigger a browser download.
functions.preCondition: AuditLogPage has loaded one or more log rows and the browser supports Blob and URL APIs.

functions.name: Server Export Endpoint Availability
functions.description: Confirm whether the backend exposes a matching /audit-logs/export route for end-to-end admin export requests.
functions.preCondition: The backend audit-log controller and service can be inspected or executed.

testCases.description: Build a blob download request for GET /audit-logs/export when the admin chooses the API export path.
testCases.procedure:
1. Call auditLogsApi.export with page, limit, action, dateFrom, dateTo, and riskLevel filters.
2. Inspect the shared apiClient.get invocation.
3. Verify the endpoint path and request options.
testCases.expectedResults:
1. auditLogsApi.export calls GET /audit-logs/export.
2. The provided filter object is passed as params without local transformation.
3. responseType is configured as blob so the caller can download binary content.
testCases.dependencies: Frontend module import resolution and shared API client are available.

testCases.description: Surface the current integration gap when the frontend expects /audit-logs/export but the backend controller exposes only GET /audit-logs.
testCases.procedure:
1. Compare the frontend export endpoint constant with AuditLogsController routes.
2. Attempt an end-to-end export call in an integrated environment.
3. Observe the HTTP outcome.
testCases.expectedResults:
1. The client requests GET /audit-logs/export.
2. The inspected backend controller does not define a matching export route.
3. End-to-end export through auditLogsApi.export fails until the server endpoint is implemented.
testCases.dependencies: Frontend and backend are running against the same API base URL.
testCases.note: This is a code-traceability gap case, not a confirmed executed HTTP failure.

testCases.description: Download the currently visible logs as formatted JSON from the page header Export button.
testCases.procedure:
1. Render AuditLogPage with logs already loaded into state.
2. Click the Export button.
3. Inspect the generated blob, object URL, and anchor download behavior.
testCases.expectedResults:
1. handleExport serializes the current logs array with JSON.stringify(logs, null, 2).
2. A Blob with application/json content is created and assigned to an object URL.
3. An anchor is created with file name audit-logs-<ISO timestamp>.json and clicked.
4. URL.revokeObjectURL is called after the download trigger.
testCases.dependencies: Browser supports Blob, URL.createObjectURL, and anchor click download behavior.

testCases.description: Keep the page stable if client-side export throws while creating the JSON download.
testCases.procedure:
1. Render AuditLogPage.
2. Force Blob or URL.createObjectURL to throw during handleExport.
3. Click Export and observe the page.
testCases.expectedResults:
1. handleExport catches the thrown error.
2. The error is logged to the console as Export failed.
3. No additional page error state is introduced and the screen remains usable.
testCases.dependencies: A test harness can mock Blob or URL.createObjectURL failures.
testCases.note: Covers the abnormal catch branch in the implemented client-only export flow.

testCases.description: Verify that the backend audit-log module currently lacks a dedicated export controller route and executable export service path.
testCases.procedure:
1. Inspect AuditLogsController and AuditLogsService.
2. Search for export-specific controller methods or response streaming logic.
3. Compare the result to the frontend expectation.
testCases.expectedResults:
1. AuditLogsController exposes only findAll via GET /audit-logs.
2. AuditLogsService contains logging and list-query behavior but no file export method.
3. Server-side Admin Log Export remains incomplete and must be implemented before the API wrapper can succeed end to end.
testCases.dependencies: Backend source files are readable in the current workspace.
testCases.note: Thin placeholder Jest specs were scanned but do not provide export execution evidence.
